Zeus.entrance: arrives in a thunderclap, the torches guttering in his presence

arrival: appears in a bolt of lightning above the keep, his voice shaking the very stones
descends on a storm cloud, beard crackling with restrained thunder, eyes judging all they see
sits upon the empty throne without asking, as if it has always been his

Hera.entrance: arrives in a procession of peacocks, surveying the disorder of your household

arrival: walks through your halls touching each doorframe, assessing the quality of your rule
examines the keep's ledgers with a disapproving eye, but says nothing — yet
offers you a marriage alliance, her smile not quite reaching her eyes

Poseidon.entrance: rises from the sea cliffs below the keep, water streaming from his beard

arrival: appears in a spray of salt water on the battlements, smelling of the deep
speaks of a treasure hidden in the sea — and a monster guarding it
warns of an earthquake coming, his trident tapping impatiently on the stone

Demeter.entrance: walks through your barren fields, and where she steps, green shoots rise

arrival: appears in the courtyard, the mud drying beneath her feet, the air smelling of spring
offers you a single seed — plant it, and your land will prosper, but it requires sacrifice
mourns the dying orchard at the edge of your land; she can save it, for a price

Athena.entrance: appears as an owl on your windowsill, then steps into the room as a woman armored in thought

arrival: arrives without announcement, already seated at your strategy table, studying your maps
speaks in riddles about your father's court — each word a key to a lock you didn't know existed
tests you with a question that has no right answer, only a true one

Apollo.entrance: arrives at dawn, golden light spilling over the walls, and sings a prophecy

arrival: appears as the sun crests the horizon, his voice carrying the weight of things that will be
offers to illuminate the dark corners of your lineage — but warns the truth may burn
challenges you to create something beautiful before sunset, his eyes bright with expectation

Artemis.entrance: appears at the edge of the forest, silver bow in hand, tracking something in the dark

arrival: arrives without sound, already standing behind you when you turn
offers to teach you to hunt — not deer, but the traitors hiding in your own court
warns of a beast in the woods that has been killing villagers. She will help you track it — if you are worthy.

Hephaestus.entrance: sets up his anvil in your courtyard without asking, the fire already burning

arrival: arrives with the smell of smoke and hot iron, already inspecting your garrison's weapons with disgust
offers to forge you a crown — but it requires metal from each of the other eleven gods
inspects the keep's defenses with a grunt; they are inadequate. He can fix them. It will cost you.

Aphrodite.entrance: appears in your mirror, more beautiful than anything you've seen, and asks if you feel worthy

arrival: arrives without warning, already seated by your fire, warming her hands as if she's always been there
offers to make you irresistible to your enemies — but warns that love is a weapon that cuts both ways
whispers that one of your advisors is in love with you, and one plans to betray you. She knows which is which.

Ares.entrance: arrives in full battle armor, bloodied from some unseen conflict, and demands your swords

arrival: kicks open the gate, breathing hard, grinning at the sight of your meager garrison
challenges you to single combat before he even tells you his name
warns of an invasion coming from the south. He will fight beside you — if you prove you can fight at all.

Hermes.entrance: appears suddenly behind you, out of breath, carrying a sealed scroll from Olympus

arrival: arrives mid-sentence, already talking, having started the conversation three miles away
offers to deliver a message to any god in the pantheon — but his price is a secret you hold
challenges you to a race across the keep's walls. If you win, he owes you a favor. If you lose, you owe him.

Persephone.entrance: rises from a crack in the keep's foundation, smelling of pomegranate and grave soil

arrival: appears at midnight, the torches dimming, the temperature dropping as she steps into the room
offers you a glimpse of your dead mother — but you must hold her hand and then let it go
warns that your father's death is coming sooner than anyone thinks. She knows the hour. She will not tell you.
